meta.description: MSM Portal design tokens. All accessed via theme.mcui.* in styled-components.

color.description: Semantic color system for AI agents. Tokens are classified by tier and grouped with their state variants.

classification.core: High-frequency tokens used across the codebase. Agents should prefer these as defaults.
classification.contextual: Used in specific components or contexts. Agents should use only when working on those components.
classification.library_internal: Used internally by @moloco/moloco-cloud-react-ui. Agents must NOT reference directly — the component library handles these.
classification.deprecated: Do not use. Migrate to the specified replacement token.

aliases.reason: Same foundation.assent token for strong selection indicators

tokens.name: text.neutral.default
tokens.$description: Primary text color for all body content, headings, labels, and data values on light backgrounds. This is the default foreground color and should be used for any text that needs maximum readability.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Placeholder text or disabled labels (use text.disabled), secondary/subdued labels (use text.neutral.subtle), text on dark or brand-colored backgrounds (use text.inverse)

tokens.name: text.neutral.subtle
tokens.usage: Labels, hints, subdued text, form field labels
tokens.$description: Secondary text color for supporting content that should be visually subordinate to primary text. Use for form field labels, hint text, descriptions, timestamps, metadata, and any content that provides context but is not the primary focus.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary body text or headings (use text.neutral.default), disabled text (use text.disabled), interactive/clickable text (use text.brand.default)

tokens.name: text.brand
tokens.$description: Brand-colored text for interactive elements that invite user action. Use for inline text links, clickable labels, and brand-emphasized text. Signals interactivity to the user.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Non-interactive emphasized text (use text.neutral.default with bold weight), error states (use text.danger.default), informational messages (use text.information.default)

hovered.$description: Hover state for brand-colored text. Applied on mouse-over for interactive text elements like clickable labels, action text, and brand-emphasized links. Shares hex with text.link.hovered for visual consistency.

pressed.$description: Pressed/active state for brand-colored text. Applied on mousedown for interactive text elements. Darkest brand text color for tactile feedback.

tokens.name: text.danger.default
tokens.$description: Danger/error text color for validation errors, destructive action labels, and critical warnings. Use inside form field error messages, delete confirmation text, and alert banners with error severity.
tokens.do_not_use_for: General warning messages (use text.warning.default), informational messages (use text.information.default), disabled destructive buttons (use text.disabled)

tokens.name: text.success.default
tokens.$description: Success/positive text color for confirmation messages, positive status indicators, and completion states. Use in success banners, status badges showing healthy/active states, and post-action confirmation text.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Brand-colored interactive text (use text.brand.default), informational status (use text.information.default), general body text (use text.neutral.default)

tokens.name: text.warning.default
tokens.$description: Warning/caution text color for non-critical alerts that require user attention. Use in warning banners, deprecation notices, approaching-limit indicators, and cautionary inline messages.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Error/validation messages (use text.danger.default), informational messages (use text.information.default), brand emphasis (use text.brand.default)
tokens.wcag_note: Contrast ratio ~1.7:1 on white backgrounds. Use only on dark backgrounds or with large text (18px+ / 14px+ bold) per WCAG AA.

tokens.name: text.information.default
tokens.$description: Informational text color for neutral-toned guidance, tips, and contextual help. Use in info banners, tooltip content emphasis, and help section callouts. Distinct from brand blue to avoid implying interactivity.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Clickable links or interactive text (use text.brand.default), error messages (use text.danger.default), general body text (use text.neutral.default)

tokens.name: text.disabled
tokens.$description: Disabled/placeholder text color for elements that are not currently interactive or have no value. Use for disabled button labels, disabled input text, placeholder text inside empty inputs, and any UI text that indicates unavailability.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Subtle but readable secondary text (use text.neutral.subtle), active body text of any kind (use text.neutral.default), error states (use text.danger.default)

tokens.name: text.inverse
tokens.$description: Inverse text color for use on dark or brand-colored backgrounds where standard dark text would be illegible. Use for text on bg.brand.default, bg.danger.bold, bg.success.bold, and other bold/dark surfaces.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Text on light/white backgrounds (use text.neutral.default), text on subtle tinted backgrounds like bg.danger.default (use text.danger.default)

tokens.name: text.neutral.grey
tokens.usage: Grey text for very subdued content
tokens.$description: Very light grey text for extremely de-emphasized content. Lighter than text.neutral.subtle.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary or secondary text (use text.neutral.default or text.neutral.subtle), disabled text (use text.disabled)

tokens.name: text.neutral.blueGrey
tokens.usage: Blue-grey text for navigation and secondary UI
tokens.$description: Blue-grey tinted text for navigation items and secondary UI content that benefits from a cooler tone.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary body text (use text.neutral.default), brand/interactive text (use text.brand.default)

tokens.name: bg.neutral
tokens.$description: Primary white surface for all main content areas. Use as the default background for pages, panels, cards, headers, popovers, and any content container that sits at the base elevation level.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Recessed/sunken areas (use bg.neutral.subtle), hover feedback on surfaces (use bg.neutral.hovered), form input fields (use bg.input.default)

hovered.$description: Hover feedback background applied when the user mouses over a neutral interactive surface. Use for table row hover, list item hover, and any clickable surface that starts with bg.neutral.default.

pressed.$description: Pressed/active feedback background applied on mousedown or active state for neutral interactive surfaces. Provides tactile feedback darker than hover.

tokens.name: bg.neutral.subtle
tokens.$description: Slightly tinted surface for areas that should appear recessed or secondary relative to the main content. Use for page backgrounds behind panels, sidebar backgrounds, and secondary content regions.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary content panels (use bg.neutral.default), hover states (use bg.neutral.hovered), disabled elements (use bg.disabled)

tokens.name: bg.neutral.subtler
tokens.usage: Hover states, guide messages, subtle fills
tokens.$description: Tertiary surface for subtle visual fills that need slightly more contrast than bg.neutral.subtle. Use for guide message backgrounds, code block backgrounds, and zebra-striped table rows.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Interactive hover states on white surfaces (use bg.neutral.hovered), disabled backgrounds (use bg.disabled), primary surfaces (use bg.neutral.default)

tokens.name: bg.transparent
tokens.$description: Fully transparent background for elements that should inherit the parent surface color. Use for ghost/text/outlined buttons, transparent overlays, and elements that blend into their container.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Content panels or cards that need visible boundaries (use bg.neutral.default), disabled backgrounds (use bg.disabled), borderless buttons with grey fill (use bg.neutral.subtle)

tokens.name: bg.brand
tokens.$description: Primary brand surface for contained/filled interactive elements. Use for primary action buttons, active tab indicators, and key call-to-action surfaces. Text on this surface must use text.inverse.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Large surface areas like page backgrounds (use bg.neutral.default), text color (use text.brand.default), subtle brand tinting (use bg.selected.default)

hovered.$description: Hover state for brand-colored surfaces. Applied automatically when user mouses over elements using bg.brand.default. Slightly darker to provide feedback.
hovered.known_misspelling: Library runtime path uses 'assent' (should be 'accent'). Do not rename — changing the path would break all consumers.

pressed.$description: Pressed/active state for brand-colored surfaces. Darkest brand surface, applied on mousedown to confirm click.
pressed.known_misspelling: Library runtime path uses 'assent' (should be 'accent'). Do not rename — changing the path would break all consumers.

tokens.known_misspelling: Library runtime path uses 'assent' (should be 'accent'). Do not rename — changing the path would break all consumers.

tokens.name: bg.danger.default
tokens.$description: Light danger tint surface for error-related containers. Use for error alert backgrounds, error banner backgrounds, and inline error callouts. Text on this surface should use text.danger.default.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Destructive button backgrounds (use bg.danger.bold), success/warning/info banners (use their respective bg tokens), general page backgrounds (use bg.neutral.default)

tokens.name: bg.danger.bold
tokens.$description: Bold/saturated danger surface for destructive action buttons and critical status indicators. Text on this surface must use text.inverse for contrast.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Error message containers or banners (use bg.danger.default), text color (use text.danger.default), warning states (use bg.warning.bold)

hovered.$description: Hover state for bold danger surfaces. Applied when user mouses over destructive buttons or critical action elements. Darker red to provide feedback.

pressed.$description: Pressed/active state for bold danger surfaces. Darkest danger surface, applied on mousedown to confirm destructive action.

tokens.name: bg.success.default
tokens.$description: Light success tint surface for positive-state containers. Use for success alert backgrounds, confirmation banners, and positive status callouts. Text on this surface should use text.success.default.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Bold success indicators (use bg.success.bold), brand surfaces (use bg.brand.default), informational banners (use bg.information.default)

tokens.name: bg.success.bold
tokens.$description: Bold/saturated success surface for strong positive indicators. Text on this surface must use text.inverse for contrast.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Success banners or alerts (use bg.success.default), text color (use text.success.default), brand actions (use bg.brand.default)

tokens.name: bg.warning.default
tokens.$description: Light warning tint surface for cautionary containers. Use for warning alert backgrounds, deprecation notice banners, and limit-approaching callouts. Text on this surface should use text.warning.default.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Error/danger banners (use bg.danger.default), bold warning indicators (use bg.warning.bold), general page backgrounds (use bg.neutral.default)

tokens.name: bg.warning.bold
tokens.$description: Bold/saturated warning surface for strong cautionary indicators. Text on this surface must use text.inverse for contrast.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Warning banners or alerts (use bg.warning.default), text color (use text.warning.default), error indicators (use bg.danger.bold)

tokens.name: bg.information.default
tokens.$description: Light informational tint surface for neutral guidance containers. Use for info alert backgrounds, help banners, and contextual tip callouts. Text on this surface should use text.information.default.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Brand surfaces (use bg.brand.default), bold info indicators (use bg.information.bold), success/warning/error banners (use their respective tokens)

tokens.name: bg.information.bold
tokens.$description: Bold/saturated informational surface for strong info indicators. Text on this surface must use text.inverse for contrast.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Info banners or alerts (use bg.information.default), text color (use text.information.default), brand actions (use bg.brand.default)

tokens.name: bg.information.subtle
tokens.usage: Subtle info background, tooltips, help sections
tokens.$description: Lighter informational tint using BLUE[100]. Subtler than bg.information.default (#E1F5FE). Use for tooltips, help sections, and inline info callouts that need less visual weight than full info banners.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Info banners and alerts (use bg.information.default), error/danger backgrounds (use bg.danger.default), general surfaces (use bg.neutral.default)

tokens.name: bg.disabled
tokens.$description: Disabled surface for elements that are not currently interactive. Use for disabled buttons, disabled cards, and any container whose content is unavailable. Pair with text.disabled and border.disabled.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Subtle secondary backgrounds (use bg.neutral.subtle), hover states (use bg.neutral.hovered), disabled form inputs specifically (use bg.input.disabled)

tokens.name: bg.selected
tokens.$description: Light brand-tinted surface for selected/active items in lists, tables, and navigation. Use for selected table rows, active navigation items, and chosen options in selection lists.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Hover state (use bg.neutral.hovered), brand button backgrounds (use bg.brand.default), informational tints (use bg.information.default)

hovered.$description: Hover feedback for already-selected items. Applied when user mouses over a selected table row, active nav item, or chosen list option. Slightly darker than bg.selected.default to provide compound state feedback (selected + hovered).

tokens.name: bg.input
tokens.$description: Default background for form input fields in their resting state. Use for text inputs, textareas, selects, and other form controls. Visually white but semantically distinct from bg.neutral.default to allow independent theming.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Page or panel backgrounds (use bg.neutral.default), disabled inputs (use bg.input.disabled), non-form content areas

hovered.$description: Hover state background for form input fields. Applied when the user mouses over an enabled form control to provide subtle feedback.

disabled.$description: Disabled background for form input fields that cannot be edited. Pair with text.disabled for the input value and border.disabled for the border.

tokens.name: border.neutral.default
tokens.$description: Primary border color for structural boundaries like panel edges, card borders, horizontal dividers, and table cell borders. The most common border token in the system.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Form input borders — use border.input.default instead (same value today, may diverge in future themes)

tokens.name: border.neutral.subtle
tokens.$description: Lighter border for subtle visual separation where a full-weight border would be too prominent. Use for inner section dividers, nested separators within panels, and lightweight structural lines.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary panel or card borders (use border.neutral.default), form input borders (use border.input.default), focus or active state borders (use border.brand.default)

tokens.name: border.brand.default
tokens.usage: Focus rings, active state borders, selected borders
tokens.$description: Brand-colored border for focus indicators, active/selected element outlines, and emphasis borders. Use for keyboard focus rings on interactive elements, active tab indicators, and selected card outlines.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard structural borders (use border.neutral.default), error state borders (use border.danger.default), disabled borders (use border.disabled)
tokens.known_misspelling: Library runtime path uses 'assent' (should be 'accent'). Do not rename — changing the path would break all consumers.

tokens.name: border.danger.default
tokens.$description: Danger/error border for elements in an error state. Use on form inputs that have failed validation, error alert container borders, and destructive action outlines.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard borders (use border.neutral.default), warning states (use border.warning.default), focus rings on valid elements (use border.brand.default)

tokens.name: border.success.default
tokens.$description: Success/positive border for elements in a confirmed or valid state. Use on success alert container borders and validated input outlines.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard borders (use border.neutral.default), brand/focus rings (use border.brand.default), informational states (use border.brand.default)

tokens.name: border.warning.default
tokens.$description: Warning/caution border for elements requiring user attention. Use on warning alert container borders and cautionary input outlines.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Error/danger borders (use border.danger.default), standard borders (use border.neutral.default), brand emphasis (use border.brand.default)

tokens.name: border.warning.subtle
tokens.$description: Light warning border for warning containers and callouts. Lighter than border.warning.default (foundation.warning).
tokens.do_not_use_for: Error borders (use border.danger.default), standard borders (use border.neutral.default)

tokens.name: border.black
tokens.$description: Pure black border for maximum contrast edges.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard panel borders (use border.neutral.default), subtle separators (use border.neutral.subtle)

tokens.name: border.disabled
tokens.$description: Disabled border for elements that are not interactive. Pair with bg.disabled and text.disabled for a consistent disabled appearance.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Subtle structural borders (use border.neutral.subtle), standard resting borders (use border.neutral.default), disabled form input borders specifically (use border.input.default with disabled styling)

tokens.name: border.input
tokens.$description: Default border for form input controls in their resting state. Semantically distinct from border.neutral.default to allow independent theming of form controls. Use for text inputs, selects, textareas, and number inputs.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Panel or card borders (use border.neutral.default), focused input state (use border.input.focused), error input state (use border.input.error)

focused.$description: Focus state border for form inputs when the user clicks into or tabs to the field. Replaces border.input.default on focus. Visually identical to border.brand.default but semantically reserved for form input focus.

error.$description: Error state border for form inputs that have failed validation. Replaces border.input.default when the field has a Formik/Yup validation error. Pair with text.danger.default for the error message text.

tokens.note: Same hex as border.neutral.default today. Separated for future theme divergence. Use this for form input borders specifically.

tokens.name: icon.neutral
tokens.$description: Primary icon color for icons accompanying body text, navigation icons, and action icons on light backgrounds. This is the default and most common icon color.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Icons that should appear subdued or secondary (use icon.neutral.subtle), icons on dark/brand backgrounds (use icon.inverse), disabled icons (use icon.disabled)

hovered.$description: Hover state for primary icons. Darker blue-grey than the default to provide feedback.

tokens.name: icon.neutral.subtle
tokens.$description: Secondary icon color for icons that should be visually subordinate. Use for decorative icons, trailing action icons in lists, and icons in secondary UI areas where full contrast is not needed.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary navigation or action icons (use icon.neutral.default), brand/interactive icons (use icon.brand.default), disabled icons (use icon.disabled)

tokens.name: icon.brand
tokens.$description: Brand-colored icon for interactive, accent, and active/selected state purposes. Use for icons that signal interactivity (add, edit, link), brand-emphasized decorative icons, active navigation indicators, and selected toggle states. Previously separate 'icon.active' token was merged here as both shared the same hex (#346bea) and overlapping semantics.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard non-interactive icons (use icon.neutral.default), error-related icons (use icon.danger.default), icons on brand backgrounds (use icon.inverse), inactive/resting navigation icons (use icon.primaryNav)

hovered.$description: Hover state for brand-colored icons. Applied on mouse-over for interactive icons like add, edit, and link icons. Darker blue to provide feedback.

tokens.name: icon.danger
tokens.$description: Critical/error icon color for icons in error and critical alert contexts. Alias for icon.critical.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Warning-level icons (use icon.warning.default), informational icons (use icon.information.default)

hovered.$description: Hover state for critical/danger icons. Darker red for feedback.

tokens.name: icon.action
tokens.$description: Light grey icon for action/toolbar icons in a subdued state.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary navigation icons (use icon.neutral.default), brand icons (use icon.brand.default)

tokens.name: icon.draft
tokens.$description: Light grey icon for draft/inactive status indicators.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Active status icons (use icon.brand.default or icon.success.default)

tokens.name: icon.primaryNav
tokens.$description: Blue-grey icon for primary navigation sidebar icons.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Content area icons (use icon.neutral.default), brand/interactive icons (use icon.brand.default)

tokens.name: icon.black
tokens.$description: Pure black icon for maximum contrast.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard icons (use icon.neutral.default), icons on dark backgrounds (use icon.inverse)

tokens.name: icon.success.default
tokens.$description: Success/positive icon color for icons accompanying success messages and positive status indicators. Use for checkmark icons in success alerts, active status dots, and completion indicators.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Brand/interactive icons (use icon.brand.default), informational icons (use icon.information.default), general decorative icons (use icon.neutral.default)

tokens.name: icon.warning.default
tokens.$description: Warning/caution icon color for icons accompanying warning messages and attention-required states. Use for warning alert icons, approaching-limit indicators, and deprecation notice icons.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Error/critical icons (use icon.danger.default), informational icons (use icon.information.default), general icons (use icon.neutral.default)

tokens.name: icon.information.default
tokens.$description: Informational icon color for icons accompanying info messages, help tooltips, and contextual guidance. Distinct from icon.brand.default to avoid implying interactivity.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Interactive/clickable icons (use icon.brand.default), error icons (use icon.danger.default), general navigation icons (use icon.neutral.default)

tokens.name: icon.disabled
tokens.$description: Disabled icon color for icons within disabled elements. Pair with text.disabled and bg.disabled for a consistent disabled appearance across the element.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Subtle secondary icons that are still interactive (use icon.neutral.subtle), active icons of any kind (use icon.neutral.default), decorative low-emphasis icons (use icon.neutral.subtle)

tokens.name: icon.inverse
tokens.$description: Inverse icon color for icons placed on dark or brand-colored surfaces. Use for icons inside contained brand buttons, icons on bold status surfaces (bg.danger.bold, bg.success.bold), and icons on dark overlays.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Icons on light backgrounds (use icon.neutral.default), icons on subtle tinted backgrounds like bg.danger.default (use icon.danger.default), disabled icons on dark surfaces (not applicable)

deprecated.name: icon.active
deprecated.$description: DEPRECATED: Merged into icon.brand.default. Both shared #346bea and overlapping active/selected semantics. Use icon.brand.default for all brand-colored and active/selected state icons.
deprecated.do_not_use_for: All new code — use icon.brand.default instead

$deprecated.reason: Merged into icon.brand.default — both shared #346bea and overlapping active/selected semantics

codebase_deviations.description: Tokens found in the codebase that are NOT part of the design system. Agents must NOT use these — use the correct replacement instead.

deviations.reason: Wrong namespace — palette.text.* does not exist, should be palette.content.*
Legacy token name from older version
Material UI alias — not part of Moloco design system
Undocumented border token — use border.brand.default for selected borders
Non-existent palette category — likely a bug

colorPalette.description: Base color palette. Semantic tokens reference these values.

families.name: Blue Grey
families.description: Muted blue-grey for icons, navigation, secondary UI

elevation.description: Elevation system combining surfaces and shadows to create depth hierarchy.

levels.name: sunken
levels.usage: Page backgrounds, sidebar backgrounds, recessed areas

levels.name: default
levels.usage: Base content areas, cards at rest, panels

elevation.usage: Apply surface color as background-color and shadow as box-shadow. Higher elevation = closer to user.

spacing.description: Function-based spacing system. Base unit is 8px.
spacing.multiArgument: theme.mcui.spacing(v, h) or spacing(t, r, b, l) like CSS shorthand

values.usage: Tight gap between icon and text
values.$description: Smallest spacing unit for tight inline gaps. Use between an icon and its adjacent label text, between badge text and icon, and for compact internal padding in small components. Do not use for structural spacing between separate elements.

Base unit spacing for small internal padding and icon gaps. Use for button internal padding (top/bottom), spacing between horizontally adjacent small elements, and tight list item padding. The fundamental building block of the spacing system.

Intermediate spacing for form field internal padding and small container insets. Use for input field left/right padding, small card insets, and the gap between vertically stacked form fields within a tight group.

Standard container padding and the most common inset value. Use for card/panel internal padding, table row padding, list item padding, and the gap between form field rows. The go-to spacing for general-purpose padding.
Section-level vertical spacing for gaps between logical groups. Use for margin-bottom between form sections, gap between stacked panels, and spacing between a heading and its content block.

values.usage: Large section gaps, form action padding
values.$description: Large vertical spacing for major section boundaries and action bar padding. Use for the gap between the form body and action buttons, spacing between major page regions, and large card gaps in grid layouts.
Extra-large spacing for prominent visual separation. Use for the margin below a page or form title before content begins, and for generous vertical separation between unrelated page regions.
Largest standard spacing for generous container padding. Use for MCFormPanel internal padding, page-level horizontal margins, and any container requiring spacious breathing room around its content.
Extra-large spacing for maximum separation between major layout regions. Use for very generous page margins or large visual breaks between unrelated sections.

inline.description: Spacing within elements: icon-text gaps, button internal padding, input internal padding

inset.description: Container padding: card padding, input padding, section insets

stack.description: Vertical gaps between elements: form field gaps, section gaps, panel gaps

layout.description: Page-level spacing: section margins, page padding, large gaps

typography.description: Typography scale. Each token has size, fontWeight, lineHeight properties.

tokens.name: H_1
tokens.$description: Largest heading for dashboard numbers and display text. Use for prominent numerical values on dashboards.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Panel or card titles within a page (use H_3), section headings below page title (use H_2), any repeated heading on the same page

tokens.name: H_2
tokens.$description: Second-level heading for page titles. Use for the main page title identifying the current view.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Panel/card titles within sections (use H_3), body text emphasis (use BODY_1_BODY with bold weight)

tokens.name: H_2_SPECIAL
tokens.$description: Medium-weight variant of H_2 for overview page titles that need slightly more emphasis than standard H_2.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard page titles (use H_2), panel titles (use H_3)

tokens.name: H_3
tokens.usage: Section title, panel titles (MCFormPanelTitle), form titles
tokens.$description: Third-level heading for section and panel titles. The most frequently used heading in form pages. Use for MCFormPanel titles, dialog titles, and any titled content block within a section.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Page-level title (use H_1 or H_2), small inline labels (use H_5 or BODY_1_BODY), body text (use BODY_1_BODY)

tokens.name: H_4
tokens.$description: Fourth-level heading for sub-sections within panels or cards. Use when a panel contains multiple grouped areas that each need a label, or for sidebar section titles.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Panel-level titles (use H_3), bold inline labels (use H_5), standard body text (use BODY_1_BODY)

tokens.name: H_4_SPECIAL
tokens.$description: Regular-weight variant of H_4 for dialog body text and lighter sub-section content.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Sub-section titles needing emphasis (use H_4), body text (use BODY_1_BODY)

tokens.name: H_5
tokens.usage: Selected menu, dashboard small number, bold labels (SCBoldLabel)
tokens.$description: Smallest heading scale, same size as body text but bold. Use for inline bold labels, table column headers, selected menu items, and small titled groups within forms.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Panel or card titles (use H_3 or H_4), standard form field labels (use BODY_1_BODY), body text paragraphs (use BODY_1_BODY)

tokens.name: BUTTON_DEFAULT
tokens.$description: Typography for standard-sized button labels.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Large or small button text (use BUTTON_LARGE or BUTTON_SMALL), body text (use BODY_1_BODY)

tokens.name: BUTTON_LARGE
tokens.$description: Typography for large button labels with explicit line height.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard or small buttons (use BUTTON_DEFAULT or BUTTON_SMALL)

tokens.name: BUTTON_SMALL
tokens.$description: Typography for small button labels.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Standard or large buttons (use BUTTON_DEFAULT or BUTTON_LARGE)

tokens.name: BODY_1_BODY
tokens.usage: Standard body text, form field labels, readonly input text
tokens.$description: Primary body text scale for all standard content. The default for form field labels, input text, table cell values, button labels, and paragraph content. This is the most used typography token.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Headings or titles (use H_1 through H_5), secondary/helper text (use BODY_2), error messages (use BODY_2_SPECIAL), captions (use BODY_3)

tokens.name: BODY_1_PARAGRAPH
tokens.usage: Paragraph body text with generous line height
tokens.$description: Body text variant with increased line height for multi-line paragraph content. Use for long-form text, descriptions, and content blocks requiring better readability.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Single-line labels (use BODY_1_BODY), headings (use H_1 through H_5)

tokens.name: BODY_2
tokens.$description: Secondary body text for supporting content that supplements the primary text. Use for form field hints, descriptions below headings, metadata, timestamps, and any content that should be visually smaller than BODY_1_BODY.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary body text or form labels (use BODY_1_BODY), error messages (use BODY_2_SPECIAL), captions or extremely small text (use BODY_3), headings (use H_5)

tokens.name: BODY_2_SPECIAL
tokens.usage: Table body, LNB, small button text, error messages (MCFormFieldError)
tokens.$description: Specialized small text for table body content, left navigation bar items, and small button text. Regular weight (400) variant of BODY_2 with tighter line height.
tokens.do_not_use_for: General helper/hint text (use BODY_2), standard body content (use BODY_1_BODY), captions or labels (use BODY_3 or BODY_2)

tokens.name: BODY_3
tokens.usage: Small text, captions, group labels in action menus
tokens.$description: Small caption text with medium weight and letter spacing. Use sparingly for action menu group labels, data table footnotes, and fine print.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Primary or secondary body text (use BODY_1_BODY or BODY_2), form field labels (use BODY_1_BODY), error messages (use BODY_2_SPECIAL), headings of any level

tokens.name: BODY_4
tokens.$description: Very small text for micro labels and compact UI elements. Use sparingly.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Any readable body content (use BODY_1_BODY or BODY_2), captions (use BODY_3)

tokens.name: BODY_5
tokens.$description: Smallest text size in the system. Use extremely sparingly for legal fine print or compact data labels.
tokens.do_not_use_for: Any user-facing readable content

accessibility: Use only one H_1 per page
Do not skip heading levels (e.g., H_1 → H_3)
Minimum body text size: BODY_2 (12px) for readability
Ensure sufficient contrast: text on backgrounds must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 ratio)
bestPractices: Use H_3 for panel/card titles within MCFormPanel
Use BODY_1_BODY for form labels and standard content
Use BODY_2 for hints, descriptions, and secondary information
Use BODY_2_SPECIAL only for form validation error messages
Use BODY_3 sparingly — only for captions and tertiary information

fontFamily.usage: Applied globally in MCGlobalStyle to html and body
fontFamily.fallback: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif

borderRadius.description: Border radius tokens for consistent corner rounding.

tokens.name: radius.small
tokens.$description: Minimal corner rounding for large containers and surfaces where sharp corners are preferred but a slight softening is needed. Use for form panels, guide message containers, and banner edges. Provides structure without appearing overly rounded.

tokens.name: radius.default
tokens.usage: Buttons, inputs, popovers, cards, action items
tokens.$description: Standard corner rounding for most interactive and contained elements. The most commonly used radius in the system. Use for buttons, form inputs, select dropdowns, popover containers, cards, tooltips, and action menu items.

tokens.name: radius.large
tokens.$description: Larger corner rounding for prominent overlay containers and featured content blocks. Use for modal dialogs (MCCommonDialog), large feature cards, and any container that should feel visually distinct and elevated.

tokens.name: radius.circle
tokens.$description: Fully circular rounding for elements that must appear as circles or pills. Use for user avatars, status indicator dots, round icon buttons, and circular badge indicators. Requires equal width and height to produce a perfect circle.

tokens.name: border.width.default
tokens.usage: Standard borders for panels, inputs, dividers

tokens.name: border.width.bold
tokens.usage: Focus rings, active tab indicator, emphasis borders

layoutConstants.description: Hardcoded layout constants defined in component files (not theme tokens)

breakpoints.description: Responsive breakpoint system. Mobile-first: apply base styles, then override at larger breakpoints.

values.name: xs
values.description: Mobile portrait and up (default base styles)

values.name: lg
values.description: Desktops, laptops — primary MSM Portal target

guidelines: MSM Portal is primarily a desktop app — design for lg (1200px) first
Use breakpoints for layout shifts (column count, sidebar visibility), not for minor spacing tweaks
Always test at lg and xl breakpoints as these are the most common screen sizes

animation.description: Motion and animation tokens for consistent transitions and micro-interactions.

durations.name: duration.fast
durations.usage: Hover states, color changes, opacity transitions

durations.name: duration.slow
durations.usage: Complex animations: modal open/close, page transitions

durations.name: duration.slower
durations.usage: Emphasis animations: skeleton loading pulse, progress bars

easings.name: easing.default
easings.usage: Standard easing for most transitions (Material ease-in-out)

guidelines: Always use named duration + easing tokens instead of arbitrary values
Respect prefers-reduced-motion: wrap animations in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)
Hover transitions should be fast (100ms) to feel responsive
Modal/dialog animations should be slow (300ms) to feel intentional
Never animate layout properties (width, height, top, left) — use transform instead

darkMode.description: Dark mode semantic token mapping. Maps each light mode semantic token to its dark mode equivalent hex value.
darkMode.note: Dark mode is not yet active in MSM Portal. These mappings are prepared for future implementation.
darkMode.activation: Will use styled-components ThemeProvider to swap theme objects based on user preference.
